subject: Add the option to clone the source code repo
commit_message:
Add the option to clone the source code repo

Change-Id: Iba5f7e5a1c6586a1a8c158854f95904944ba62c0
Issue-Id: INT-101
Signed-off-by: Idan Amit <ia096e@intl.att.com>
Co-Authored-By: Victor Morales <victor.morales@intel.com>

entries.message: Patch Set 2: Published edit on patch set 1.
Patch Set 3: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

This change won't be necessary given that the previous change clones all the repositories before compile them.

Patch Set 4:

The thing that I've tried to achieve here is that only if you want to clone you'll need to compile and vice versa. In my case we don't want the code to be on our vagrant.
I can't seem to understand what is the problem of giving the user the ability to choose if he wants to clone and compile the code or not?

Patch Set 4:

> The thing that I've tried to achieve here is that only if you want
 > to clone you'll need to compile and vice versa. In my case we don't
 > want the code to be on our vagrant.
 > I can't seem to understand what is the problem of giving the user
 > the ability to choose if he wants to clone and compile the code or
 > not?

I understand.  This approach needs to be expanded to other components, but I was thinking in the creation of other variable maybe like *clone_repo*, ending is something like

    if [[ "$clone_repo" == "True" ]]; then
        clone_all_sdc_repos
        if [[ "$compile_repo" == "True" ]]; then
            compile_all_sdc_repos
        fi
    fi

What do you think?

Patch Set 4:

the question is is their a point in separating the two variables compile and clone?
in addition i wanted to know are you aware if any one except us is using this vagrant?
and how are they working with it are they cloning into it?
and i belive that the clone flag will serve all components and allow them more flexibility to use this as they see fit.

Patch Set 5:

Hey Michael

 > the question is is their a point in separating the two variables
 > compile and clone?
The current behavior is to clone everything and only compile by demand on all the components, having the alternative(clone_sdc_repos=False and compile_sdc_repos=False) to only install dependencies won't make too much sense to me, but if that is something desired I can be implemented

 > in addition i wanted to know are you aware if any one except us is
 > using this vagrant?
Yes, I'm aware of that.  MultiVIM is planning to add support but they're not using it yet.

 > and how are they working with it are they cloning into it?
I know that few people is using it but it's not providing feedback about it. But all these functions can be consumed later, what I mean is that we can skip the cloning function during the initial provisioning process and call it later, 

$ source /var/onap/asdc
$ clone_all_sdc_repos

https://git.onap.org/integration/tree/bootstrap/vagrant-onap/doc/source/features/consuming_scripts.rst#n85

 > and i belive that the clone flag will serve all components and
 > allow them more flexibility to use this as they see fit.
+1 I can propagated that change to other modules and set default value to "True" to preserve the current behavior.
Change has been successfully merged by Gary Wu
